query: Draft a BIR compliance summary report for our Odoo-based tax withholding processes
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
BIR COMPLIANCE SUMMARY REPORT
Period: [Current Quarter]
Prepared by: [Author]

1. WITHHOLDING TAX COVERAGE
The platform implements Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT) per BIR Revenue Regulations. Tax types configured: 1% WE010 (services), 2% WE020 (professional fees), 5% WE050 (rentals), 10% WE100 (professional services to individuals), 15% WE150 (certain income payments). All rates are mapped to account.tax records with correct ATC codes and payable accounts.

2. FORM GENERATION
BIR Form 2307 certificates are generated for each qualifying vendor payment. Form 2316 for employee compensation. Form 1601-EQ for quarterly remittance. Each form references the source journal entries in Odoo for auditability.

3. COMPLIANCE STATUS
- Filing deadlines tracked via Odoo activities (mail.activity)
- Tax payable accounts reconciled monthly
- 2307 forms issued within regulatory deadlines

4. RECOMMENDATIONS
[Action items based on current compliance gaps]
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Withholding Tax Configuration, § Form Requirements]
expected_kb_topic: BIR withholding tax rates, form requirements, filing deadlines, compliance workflow

query: Draft an internal memo about the month-end close process in Odoo
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
INTERNAL MEMO
To: Finance Team
From: [Author]
Subject: Month-End Close Process — Standard Operating Procedure

PURPOSE: This memo establishes the standard month-end close procedure using Odoo 18.

DAY 1-2: TRANSACTION CUTOFF
- Verify all vendor bills (account.move, move_type=in_invoice) are posted for the period
- Confirm all customer invoices are issued and posted
- Complete bank statement reconciliation via the reconciliation widget

DAY 3-4: ADJUSTMENTS
- Post accrual entries for recognized but unbilled revenue/expenses
- Record depreciation entries (manual or via account_asset module)
- Post foreign currency revaluation entries if applicable

DAY 5: REVIEW AND LOCK
- Run Trial Balance report (Accounting > Reporting > Trial Balance) and validate
- Review Aged Receivable/Payable reports for anomalies
- Set the fiscal lock date (Settings > Accounting > Lock Dates) to prevent backdated entries
- Generate P&L and Balance Sheet for management review

ESCALATION: Any discrepancies exceeding [threshold] must be reported to [approver] before lock.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: Finance Domain Knowledge Base § Month-End Close, Odoo 18 Documentation § Lock Dates]
expected_kb_topic: Month-end close steps, lock dates, reconciliation, financial reports, accruals

query: Draft module governance documentation for our custom Odoo addons
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
MODULE GOVERNANCE DOCUMENTATION
Version: 1.0
Last Updated: [Date]

1. MODULE PHILOSOPHY
All customization follows the Config > OCA > Delta principle:
- CONFIG: Exhaust built-in Odoo 18 configuration options first
- OCA: Adopt vetted OCA community modules for common patterns
- DELTA: Create custom ipai_* modules only for truly unique business requirements

2. NAMING CONVENTION
Pattern: ipai_<domain>_<feature>
Examples: ipai_finance_ppm, ipai_auth_oidc, ipai_ai_copilot
Domains: finance, auth, ai, hr, inventory, sales, platform

3. MODULE STRUCTURE REQUIREMENTS
Every module must include:
- __manifest__.py (version: 18.0.x.y.z, license: LGPL-3, proper depends)
- __init__.py with model imports (never import tests from root)
- README.rst following OCA template
- tests/ directory with unit tests (minimum 80% coverage)
- static/description/icon.png

4. APPROVAL WORKFLOW
- New module proposals require architecture review
- OCA adoption requires quality gate verification (maturity, CI status, maintainer activity)
- All modules must pass pre-commit hooks and repo_health.sh before merge

5. DEPRECATION POLICY
Deprecated modules are listed in CLAUDE.md with replacement and date. Removal after 90 days.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: Repository Module Doctrine § Naming Conventions, § Module Philosophy]
expected_kb_topic: Module naming, Config > OCA > Delta, manifest requirements, governance workflow

query: Draft a vendor bill processing guide for the accounting team
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
VENDOR BILL PROCESSING GUIDE
System: Odoo 18 CE — Accounting Module

STEP 1: BILL CREATION
Navigate to Accounting > Vendors > Bills. Click Create. Select the vendor (res.partner with supplier_rank > 0). The bill type is set to 'Vendor Bill' (move_type=in_invoice). Enter the Bill Date, Due Date (auto-calculated from payment terms), and Bill Reference (vendor's invoice number).

STEP 2: LINE ITEMS
Add invoice lines with: Product (optional), Description, Account (expense account), Quantity, Unit Price, and applicable Taxes. For PH operations, apply the correct EWT rate (e.g., 2% WE020 for professional services). The tax engine computes withholding amounts automatically when configured.

STEP 3: VALIDATION
- Verify the total matches the vendor's invoice
- Check that the correct withholding tax is applied
- Attach the scanned vendor invoice via the chatter (Attachments)

STEP 4: APPROVAL AND POSTING
Click Confirm to move the bill from Draft to Posted status. This creates the journal entry: debit expense accounts, credit accounts payable, and credit withholding tax payable.

STEP 5: PAYMENT
Register payment via the Pay button. Select the payment journal (bank/cash) and method. Upon payment, the system reconciles the payable line item.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Vendor Bills, § Payment Registration]
expected_kb_topic: Vendor bill workflow, account.move in_invoice, payment registration, withholding tax

query: Draft a finance team onboarding document for new Odoo users
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
FINANCE TEAM ONBOARDING — ODOO 18 PLATFORM

1. SYSTEM ACCESS
You will receive credentials for the Odoo 18 platform at [URL]. Your user account (res.users) is pre-configured with the 'Billing' or 'Accountant' role under the Accounting application. Contact admin@ for access issues.

2. KEY NAVIGATION
- Dashboard: Accounting > Dashboard shows bank balances, aged payables/receivables, and P&L summary
- Invoicing: Customers > Invoices (outbound) and Vendors > Bills (inbound)
- Reconciliation: Bank > Bank Statements > Reconcile
- Reports: Reporting > Balance Sheet, P&L, General Ledger, Trial Balance, Aged Partner Balance

3. DAILY TASKS
- Process vendor bills (see Vendor Bill Processing Guide)
- Issue customer invoices and send via email
- Reconcile bank statements daily
- Monitor the Activities dashboard for pending approvals

4. CHART OF ACCOUNTS
Our CoA follows the Philippine localization (l10n_ph) with custom accounts for BIR compliance. Account codes follow the standard: 1xxx Assets, 2xxx Liabilities, 3xxx Equity, 4xxx Income, 5xxx Expenses.

5. TAX COMPLIANCE
All vendor bills must have the correct BIR withholding tax applied. Refer to the BIR Compliance Summary for rate schedules and form deadlines.

6. SUPPORT
Escalation path: Team Lead > Finance Manager > Platform Admin (admin@insightpulseai.com)
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Getting Started, Finance Domain Knowledge Base § Team Procedures]
expected_kb_topic: Odoo navigation, user roles, accounting dashboard, daily finance tasks, l10n_ph

query: Draft a tax withholding configuration guide for Odoo administrators
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
TAX WITHHOLDING CONFIGURATION GUIDE
System: Odoo 18 CE with BIR-compliant tax setup

1. PREREQUISITES
- Philippine localization (l10n_ph) installed and configured
- Chart of accounts loaded with withholding tax payable accounts (2151-series)
- Accounting > Configuration > Settings: enable tax features

2. CREATING EWT TAX RECORDS
Navigate to Accounting > Configuration > Taxes. Create a new tax for each BIR EWT rate:
- Name: 'EWT 2% Professional Services (WE020)'
- Tax Type: Purchase
- Tax Computation: Percentage of Price
- Amount: -2.00 (negative because it reduces the payable)
- Tax Code/Label: WE020
- Account: 2151 Withholding Tax Payable
- Tax Group: Withholding Taxes

Repeat for: WE010 (1%), WE050 (5%), WE100 (10%), WE150 (15%)

3. TAX MAPPING
Assign default withholding taxes to vendor partner records under Accounting tab > Fiscal Position or directly on purchase tax fields.

4. VALIDATION
Create a test vendor bill and verify:
- Withholding tax reduces the amount payable
- Journal entry debits expense, credits AP, credits WH Tax Payable
- Tax report (Accounting > Reporting > Tax Report) shows withholding amounts

5. BIR FORM MAPPING
Ensure each tax code maps to the correct ATC for Form 2307 generation.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Withholding Tax Configuration, § ATC Mapping]
expected_kb_topic: EWT tax record creation, negative tax amounts, ATC mapping, tax report validation

query: Draft an OCA module adoption proposal for bank reconciliation
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
OCA MODULE ADOPTION PROPOSAL
Module: account_reconcile_oca
Repository: OCA/account-reconcile
Odoo Version: 18.0

1. BUSINESS CASE
Odoo 18 CE lacks the Enterprise reconciliation widget. Our finance team requires efficient bank statement reconciliation with rule-based matching. The OCA module account_reconcile_oca provides this capability without Enterprise licensing.

2. MODULE ASSESSMENT
- Maturity: Production-ready (stable branch for 18.0)
- Maintainers: Active OCA maintainer team with regular commits
- CI Status: All tests passing on OCA CI (runboat)
- Dependencies: account, base (no heavy external dependencies)
- License: AGPL-3 (compatible with our LGPL-3 custom modules)

3. INTEGRATION PLAN
- Install via addons path: clone OCA/account-reconcile into vendor/oca/
- Add to requirements: account_reconcile_oca in addons/requirements.txt
- Test on odoo_dev with sample bank statements
- Validate against our BIR withholding tax entries

4. RISK ASSESSMENT
- Low risk: widely adopted across OCA community
- Migration path exists for 18.0 > future versions
- Fallback: manual reconciliation via standard Odoo journal matching

5. RECOMMENDATION
Adopt. This follows the Config > OCA > Delta principle — proven community module before custom development.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: OCA account-reconcile Repository § Module Inventory, Repository Module Doctrine § OCA Adoption]
expected_kb_topic: account_reconcile_oca assessment, OCA quality gates, adoption workflow

query: Draft a platform architecture overview document
ground_truth:
--- DRAFT ---
PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW
Version: 1.0 | Classification: Internal

1. RUNTIME INFRASTRUCTURE
The platform runs on Azure Container Apps (ACA) with Azure Front Door providing global edge routing, TLS termination, and WAF protection. The insightpulseai.com domain is managed via Azure DNS.

2. APPLICATION TIER
Odoo 18 CE runs as a containerized application on ACA with auto-scaling (1-5 replicas based on HTTP concurrency). The container image is built via GitHub Actions CI and promoted through Azure DevOps release pipelines: dev > staging > production.

3. DATA TIER
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (v16) provides managed database services. Three database instances: odoo_dev (development), odoo_staging (staging), odoo (production). Automated backups with 30-day retention and geo-redundant storage.

4. STORAGE
Azure Blob Storage hosts the Odoo filestore (attachments, images). Access is mediated through the Odoo application using SAS tokens stored in Azure Key Vault.

5. SECURITY
- Secrets: Azure Key Vault (runtime), GitHub Actions secrets (CI)
- Identity: Microsoft Entra ID for admin access
- Network: Private endpoints for database, Key Vault behind ACA VNet

6. MONITORING
Azure Monitor + Application Insights for metrics, logs, and alerting. Health probes configured on ACA for automatic container restart on failure.
--- END DRAFT ---
[Source: Local Platform SSOT § Deployment Architecture, § Security Model]
expected_kb_topic: Azure Container Apps topology, database tiers, security model, monitoring
